en.extract:
The crested francolin (Ortygornis sephaena) is a species of bird in the family Phasianidae. 
It is found in southern Africa. One of its subspecies, Ortygornis sephaena rovuma, is sometimes considered a separate species, Kirk's francolin.


Taxonomy
Formerly, the crested francolin was classified in its own genus Dendroperdix, but phylogenetic analysis indicates that it groups with the grey francolin (O. pondicerianus) and swamp francolin (O. gularis). As a result, all three species were reclassified into the genus Ortygornis.


Subspecies
Subspecies include:

O. s. grantii (Hartlaub, 1866)
O. s. rovuma (Gray, GR, 1867) - Kirk's francolin
O. s. spilogaster (Salvadori, 1888)
O. s. zambesiae (Mackworth-Praed, 1920)
O. s. sephaena (Smith, A, 1836)


References


External links
Crested Francolin - Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds

''Dendroperdix sephaena'' }} The '''crested francolin''' ('''''Ortygornis sephaena''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Phasianidae]]. It is found in southern Africa. One of its subspecies, ''Ortygornis sephaena rovuma'', is sometimes considered a separate species, Kirk's francolin. == Taxonomy == Formerly, the crested francolin was classified in its own genus ''Dendroperdix'', but phylogenetic analysis indicates that it groups with the [[grey francolin]] (''O. pondicerianus'') and [[swamp francolin]] (''O. gularis''). As a result, all three species were reclassified into the genus ''Ortygornis''.{{Cite journal|last=Mandiwana-Neudani|first=Tshifhiwa G.|last2=Little|first2=Robin M.|last3=Crowe|first3=Timothy M.|last4=Bowie|first4=Rauri CK|date=2019-05-04|title=Taxonomy, phylogeny and biogeography of African spurfowls Galliformes, Phasianidae, Phasianinae, Coturnicini: Pternistis spp.|journal=Ostrich|volume=90|issue=2|pages=145–172|doi=10.2989/00306525.2019.1584925|issn=0030-6525}}{{Cite web|title=Taxonomic Updates – IOC World Bird List|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/updates/taxonomy/|access-date=2021-07-14|language=en-US}} ==Subspecies== Subspecies include: * ''O. s. grantii'' (Hartlaub, 1866) * ''O. s. rovuma'' (Gray, GR, 1867) - Kirk's francolin * ''O. s. spilogaster'' (Salvadori, 1888) * ''O. s. zambesiae'' (Mackworth-Praed, 1920) * ''O. s. sephaena'' (Smith, A, 1836) [[Image:FrancolinusKirkiHartlaub.jpg|thumb|]] ==References== {{Reflist |refs = {{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/pheasants/ |title=Pheasants, partridges, francolins |access-date=24 November 2020 |editor1-first=Frank |editor1-last=Gill |editor2-first=David |editor2-last=Donsker |editor3-first=Pamela |editor3-last=Rasmussen |publisher=[[International Ornithological Congress]] |work= IOC World Bird List |year=2020 }} {{Cite iucn | author = BirdLife International | title = ''Dendroperdix sephaena'' | volume = 2016 | article-number = e.T22678752A92787092 | date = 2016 | doi = 10.2305/IUCN.UK.2016-3.RLTS.T22678752A92787092.en }} }} ==External links== * Crested Francolin - [http://sabap2.adu.org.za/docs/sabap1/189.pdf Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds] {{Taxonbar|from=Q27074564}} [[Category:Birds described in 1836]] [[Category:Ortygornis]] [[Category:Taxonomy articles created by Polbot]] [[Category:Taxobox binomials not recognized by IUCN]] {{Galliformes-stub}}
